publicationstmt.publisher: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

profiledesc.creation: This finding aid was produced using ArchivesSpace on 2020-08-10 18:14:16 -0400.

change.item: This resource was modified by the ArchivesSpace Preprocessor developed by the Harvard Library (https://github.com/harvard-library/archivesspace-preprocessor)

repository.corpname: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections

did.physdesc: Reports, plans, blueprints, photographs, publications, contracts, and drawings.
did.abstract: Reports and studies, including plans, blueprints, photographs, publications, contracts, and drawings pertaining to transportation, parkways, and city planning projects in Queens, Westchester County, Long Island, Buffalo, and New York, New York; Bethlehem, Pennsylvania; and other places. Also, includes planning material pertaining to the 1964 World's Fair in New York City and a report prepared for Cornell University on Cornell Heights, 1987.

defitem.label: Contact Information:
defitem.item: Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections 2B Carl A. Kroch Library Cornell University Ithaca, NY 14853 (607) 255-3530 Fax: (607) 255-9524 rareref@cornell.edu http://rmc.library.cornell.edu

bioghist.head: BIOGRAPHICAL NOTE
bioghist.p: Gilmore D. Clarke (Cornell University Class of 1913), 1892-1982, was Professor of Architecture at Cornell, 1935-1950, and Dean of the College of Architecture, 1939-1950. Michael Rapuano, 1904-1975, received an M.L.A. from Cornell in 1927.

scopecontent.head: COLLECTION DESCRIPTION
scopecontent.p: Reports and studies, including plans, blueprints, photographs, publications, contracts, and drawings pertaining to transportation, parkways, and city planning projects in Queens, Westchester County, Long Island, Buffalo, and New York, New York; Bethlehem, Pennsylvania; and other places. Also, includes planning material pertaining to the 1964 World's Fair in New York City and a report prepared for Cornell University on Cornell Heights, 1987.

prefercite.head: Cite As:
prefercite.p: Clarke and Rapuano Records, #3047. Division of Rare and Manuscript Collections, Cornell University Library.

City planning.
controlaccess.corpname: New York World\'s Fair (Location of meeting: New York, N.Y.). Date of meeting or treaty signing: (1964-1965 :.)

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Parking area bridge. Designs and estimates.

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: For the 1964 World's Fair in Queens, New York. File includes correspondence; sketches; engineering computations; plans from American Bridge (US Steel was building the structure); photos of the 1939 Perisphere foundations

did.unittitle: Jones Beach Parkway Authority: Ocean Parkway

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Shelter, pump pits, bus shelter, and lighting

did.unittitle: Water Tower - Robert Moses State Park, Fire Island New York

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Preliminary and final designs and estimates

did.unittitle: Hill to Hill Bridge and Approaches

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Reports to the Commonwealth of Pennsylvania (with plans,) originals and mock up for bridge report, structural analysis

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Borings and preliminary plans showing wall 1 and 2. Water study.

New York World's Fair: Unisphere plans

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Photostats: includes plans, sketches and for post-fair development (Previously Box 156)

did.unittitle: New York World's Fair: Unisphere plans
New York World's Fair: Unisphere plans

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Drawing of benches for the fair, layout of the fairgrounds, and for the Unisphere Foundation

scopecontent.head: Scope and Contents
scopecontent.p: Official construction drawings for the Unisphere Foundation
Signed by Robert Moses, William C. Potter, Stuart Constable, William Whipple Jr. and Gilmore D. Clarke
